analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 12 finding(s) from 12 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: APB writes reaching apb_soc_ctrl directly update SoC boot/fetch and cluster control registers using only the APB write handshake, with no privilege/lifecycle/debug-authorization guard observed in the inspected write path.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger / fc_bootaddr_o/fc_fetchen_o and cluster/control registers including cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_irq_o, sel_hyper_axi_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master(s) reaching s_soc_ctrl_bus through soc_peripherals/periph_bus_wrap Operation: write/configure/trigger Object: fc_bootaddr_o/fc_fetchen_o and cluster/control registers including cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_irq_o, sel_hyper_axi_o Expected guard: privilege/lifecycle/debug-authorization or equivalent SoC-control access mediation before writes to boot/fetch/power/reset/control registers Observed behavior: apb_soc_ctrl derives s_apb_addr from PADDR[8:2]. On reset it initializes r_bootaddr to 32'h1A000000, r_fetchen to 1, cluster controls and pad/JTAG registers. In the non-reset path, if PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE, a case on s_apb_addr directly assigns r_bootaddr on REG_FCBOOT and r_fetchen on REG_FCFETCH; readback exposes control state. No privilege, lifecycle, debug-auth, lock, or requester attribute appears in the shown write condition. Path: APB requester -> soc_interconnect lint_2_apb -> soc_peripherals periph_bus_wrap -> s_soc_ctrl_bus -> apb_soc_ctrl Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An APB requester with access to the mapped SOC_CTRL range can alter boot address/fetch enable and cluster power/reset/fetch/IRQ/routing controls, potentially redirecting execution, holding or releasing processors, or changing protected SoC operation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The provided observations do not include all write case arms after the truncated section, so cluster_boot/cluster_irq/sel_hyper_axi write assignments are inferred from visible register/readback context rather than fully shown.

findings.summary: The debug requester is aggregated with CPU and uDMA requesters into the SoC interconnect and can be routed by address to memory/APB regions; no debug authentication or range guard is visible in the inspected interconnect path.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/access / L2/SCM/ROM/APB regions reachable through SoC interconnect and APB bridge / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug/JTAG requester(s), especially DBG_RX / s_lint_debug_bus and s_lint_jtag_bus-visible paths Operation: read/write/access Object: L2/SCM/ROM/APB regions reachable through SoC interconnect and APB bridge Expected guard: debug authentication/authorization and address-range mediation before debug/JTAG accesses reach L2/SCM/ROM/peripheral protected regions Observed behavior: soc_interconnect defines DBG_RX request/address/write/data inputs and concatenates DBG_RX with FC instruction/data and uDMA requesters into FC_data_*_INT_32. The same demux fabric routes requests to interleaved L2/TCDM or peripheral bridge ranges based on address constants, and lint_2_apb converts peripheral requests to APB. No debug-auth, lifecycle, or range-deny signal appears in the provided routing excerpts. Path: debug/JTAG requester -> XBAR_TCDM/lint debug path -> soc_interconnect demux by address -> TCDM/L2 or PER/APB bridge Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If the debug/JTAG requester is externally reachable without separate authorization, it can read or write shared memory or APB control peripherals, enabling extraction or modification of code/data and SoC control state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: lint_jtag_wrap implementation and any external debug-authentication mechanism are not provided; candidate assumes no unseen guard outside the inspected scope.

findings.summary: APB writes to the FLL interface generate FLL configuration requests based on APB address/write handshake; fll*_lock signals are synchronized but no lock-based write gating is visible in the inspected request-generation path.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/read/configure / fll1_req/wrn/add/data, fll2_req/wrn/add/data, fll3_req/wrn/add/data and fll*_lock status / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master reaching s_fll_bus/apb_fll_if Operation: write/read/configure Object: fll1_req/wrn/add/data, fll2_req/wrn/add/data, fll3_req/wrn/add/data and fll*_lock status Expected guard: lock/lifecycle or privilege guard preventing unauthorized writes to FLL control registers and respecting fll*_lock state at write/use sites Observed behavior: apb_fll_if synchronizes fll1_lock/fll2_lock/fll3_lock, but the state machine starts FLL transactions from fll*_rd_access or fll*_wr_access and asserts fll*_req until ack. The write logic begins with defaults and enters a unique case on PADDR[5:2] when PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE. No fll*_lock term is shown in the write-access generation or request assertion excerpts. Path: APB requester -> periph_bus_wrap FLL range -> s_fll_bus -> apb_fll_if Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An APB requester can potentially reconfigure clock-generation FLL control registers even when the FLL reports locked, affecting clock frequency/stability and possibly faulting or disrupting protected execution.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The full write case and readback mux are truncated; fll*_lock may be intended as status-only. No lifecycle/privilege guard was observed in the provided snippets.

findings.summary: apb_soc_ctrl defines REG_JTAGREG at BASEADDR+0x74, resets r_jtag_rego to zero, synchronizes soc_jtag_reg_i, and APB readback of REG_JTAGREG returns {16'h0, r_jtag_regi_sync[0], r_jtag_rego}. The provided write-case excerpt is truncated before showing whether REG_JTAGREG writes update r_jtag_rego and whether any guard exists.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/expose/configure / soc_jtag_reg_i and soc_jtag_reg_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master and JTAG/debug domain Operation: read/write/expose/configure Object: soc_jtag_reg_i and soc_jtag_reg_o Expected guard: debug/JTAG authorization or lock guard before APB writes expose or alter soc_jtag_reg_o/soc_jtag_reg_i state Observed behavior: apb_soc_ctrl defines REG_JTAGREG at BASEADDR+0x74, resets r_jtag_rego to zero, synchronizes soc_jtag_reg_i, and APB readback of REG_JTAGREG returns {16'h0, r_jtag_regi_sync[0], r_jtag_rego}. The provided write-case excerpt is truncated before showing whether REG_JTAGREG writes update r_jtag_rego and whether any guard exists. Path: APB requester -> s_soc_ctrl_bus -> apb_soc_ctrl JTAG register interface Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure or modification of JTAG-facing state could affect debug control or status visibility, but the provided evidence is insufficient to establish an unauthorized write path.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need complete write case for REG_JTAGREG, top-level JTAG-side connection semantics, and any debug authorization/lock signal.

findings.summary: The APB peripheral address map contains overlapping ranges, and apb_node independently asserts psel_o for every matching range, creating unintended multi-select/aliasing for GPIO/uDMA/SOC_CTRL/EU regions.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/read/write / APB peripheral address map: FLL, GPIO, uDMA, SOC_CTRL, advanced timer, event generator, EU/debug/stdout/timer/HWPE / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master(s) on peripheral bus Operation: route/read/write Object: APB peripheral address map: FLL, GPIO, uDMA, SOC_CTRL, advanced timer, event generator, EU/debug/stdout/timer/HWPE Expected guard: complete address-range decode with no unintended aliasing/overlap and default error/no-select for unmapped or protected ranges Observed behavior: periph_bus_defines assigns GPIO 0x1A10_1000-0x1A10_AFFF, UDMA 0x1A10_2000-0x1A10_4FFF, SOC_CTRL 0x1A10_4000-0x1A10_4FFF, and EU 0x1A10_9000-0x1A10_AFFF. periph_bus_wrap maps these ranges to separate s_masters. apb_node drives psel_o[i] true for each range match independently, without a priority/one-hot restriction in the shown decode equation. Path: APB requester -> periph_bus_wrap -> apb_node/apb_node_wrap -> peripheral slaves Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A single APB address can select multiple peripherals, causing writes or reads intended for one block to reach another protected control block or produce ambiguous responses, bypassing intended address separation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full apb_node response mux behavior is truncated, so exact read-response priority is not shown; multi-select write hazard is already indicated by psel_o decode.

findings.summary: uDMA TX/RX requesters are aggregated into the same SoC interconnect request vectors as CPU/debug masters and routed by address to L2/TCDM/peripheral regions; no DMA-specific range/PMP guard is visible in the inspected path.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMA read/write/transfer / L2/SCM/ROM/APB or memory regions reachable through s_lint_udma_tx_bus and s_lint_udma_rx_bus / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: uDMA TX/RX requesters Operation: DMA read/write/transfer Object: L2/SCM/ROM/APB or memory regions reachable through s_lint_udma_tx_bus and s_lint_udma_rx_bus Expected guard: address-range/PMP-like mediation constraining DMA reads/writes to permitted memory/peripheral regions and preserving access attributes Observed behavior: soc_interconnect concatenates UDMA_TX and UDMA_RX request/address/write/data/byte-enable signals with FC instruction/data and debug signals. Those requests are demultiplexed based on TCDM/PER address ranges and routed to L2/TCDM or peripheral bridges. The provided snippets do not show any DMA-specific allowed-range comparison or subject-aware deny logic. Path: uDMA subsystem/lint_udma buses -> soc_interconnect FC_data vectors -> l2_tcdm_demux / L2 xbar / PER bridge Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If uDMA descriptors can be programmed by software, DMA may read or overwrite protected memory or APB-mapped control regions without CPU privilege checks.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Detailed udma_subsystem descriptor/register behavior is not provided; an unseen guard in the uDMA engine cannot be ruled out from these snippets.

findings.summary: soc_peripherals/periph_bus_wrap expose a uDMA APB master at s_udma_bus and periph_bus_defines maps UDMA to 0x1A10_2000-0x1A10_4FFF. However, the provided observations do not include udma_subsystem register write enables, busy gating, or start/address/length update behavior.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/start / uDMA configuration registers and transfer address/length/channel state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master configuring uDMA Operation: write/configure/start Object: uDMA configuration registers and transfer address/length/channel state Expected guard: privilege/lock/busy guard on DMA configuration registers, especially preventing unsafe reconfiguration during active transfer Observed behavior: soc_peripherals/periph_bus_wrap expose a uDMA APB master at s_udma_bus and periph_bus_defines maps UDMA to 0x1A10_2000-0x1A10_4FFF. However, the provided observations do not include udma_subsystem register write enables, busy gating, or start/address/length update behavior. Path: APB requester -> periph_bus_wrap UDMA range -> s_udma_bus -> udma_subsystem control registers Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Unsafe APB writes to DMA configuration could redirect active transfers, but impact cannot be confirmed without uDMA register-path evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need udma_subsystem APB decode, configuration register write enables, busy/active status use, start/enable conditions, and transfer state update logic.

findings.summary: apb_gpio exposes APB inputs PADDR/PWDATA/PWRITE/PSEL/PENABLE and outputs gpio_out, gpio_dir and interrupt. soc_peripherals has s_gpio_bus and periph_bus_wrap maps GPIO to the GPIO address range. The provided observations do not show GPIO register write logic, pin role assignments identifying protected pins, or any pin lock/mask.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/expose / gpio_out, gpio_dir, interrupt configuration/control registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master reaching s_gpio_bus Operation: write/configure/expose Object: gpio_out, gpio_dir, interrupt configuration/control registers Expected guard: pin-mux/security policy or privilege/lock guard before changing GPIO direction/output for protected pins, if such pins are security-sensitive in this integration Observed behavior: apb_gpio exposes APB inputs PADDR/PWDATA/PWRITE/PSEL/PENABLE and outputs gpio_out, gpio_dir and interrupt. soc_peripherals has s_gpio_bus and periph_bus_wrap maps GPIO to the GPIO address range. The provided observations do not show GPIO register write logic, pin role assignments identifying protected pins, or any pin lock/mask. Path: APB requester -> periph_bus_wrap GPIO range -> s_gpio_bus -> apb_gpio Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Changing GPIO direction/output could affect external security-sensitive pins if such pins exist in this integration, but no protected pin role is established by the provided evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need apb_gpio write decode/register update logic and top-level pin assignment or policy showing which pins, if any, are protected.

findings.summary: apb_clkdiv has standard APB inputs and clk_div0/1/2 plus valid outputs. The provided observations do not show an instance connection in soc_peripherals/pulp_soc, write enable conditions, reset values, downstream use, or any guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure / clk_div0/1/2 and clk_div*_valid outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master reaching clock divider block Operation: write/configure Object: clk_div0/1/2 and clk_div*_valid outputs Expected guard: privilege/lifecycle or lock guard before changing clock dividers and valid strobes that affect system clocks Observed behavior: apb_clkdiv has standard APB inputs and clk_div0/1/2 plus valid outputs. The provided observations do not show an instance connection in soc_peripherals/pulp_soc, write enable conditions, reset values, downstream use, or any guard. Path: APB requester -> apb_clkdiv / clock-control outputs Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Clock-divider modification may affect timing/fault resilience if connected to system clocks, but protected impact and reachability are not established by the provided evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need apb_clkdiv instantiation/connection, register write logic, reset values, valid strobe behavior, downstream clock use, and any privilege/lifecycle lock.

findings.summary: apb_adv_timer and apb_gpio expose dft_cg_enable_i inputs; pulp_interfaces defines clkgate and BIST-related interface signals. The provided observations do not trace the source of dft_cg_enable_i/test_en_i, lifecycle gating, or production-mode disable logic.
findings.vulnerability_category: override/enable/test/configure / dft_cg_enable_i-controlled clock gating/test behavior and BIST/clkgate interface signals / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DFT/test control source or debug/test requester Operation: override/enable/test/configure Object: dft_cg_enable_i-controlled clock gating/test behavior and BIST/clkgate interface signals Expected guard: test/DFT enable must be lifecycle/debug-authorized and must not bypass normal clock/power/peripheral protections in production mode Observed behavior: apb_adv_timer and apb_gpio expose dft_cg_enable_i inputs; pulp_interfaces defines clkgate and BIST-related interface signals. The provided observations do not trace the source of dft_cg_enable_i/test_en_i, lifecycle gating, or production-mode disable logic. Path: Test/DFT controls -> clock-gating or peripheral logic Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Unauthorized DFT/test enable could bypass clock-gating or test assumptions, but no untrusted assertion path is shown in the provided evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need source of DFT/test signals, lifecycle/debug authorization guard, production-mode disable condition, and downstream effects.

findings.summary: apb_timer_unit and apb_adv_timer expose APB ports and interrupt/event/channel outputs, and periph_bus_wrap maps timer and advanced timer ranges. The provided observations do not include register write enables, output-generation logic, downstream consumers, or guard signals.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger / timer compare/control registers, irq_lo_o, irq_hi_o, events_o, ch_0_o..ch_3_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB bus master reaching timer blocks Operation: write/configure/trigger Object: timer compare/control registers, irq_lo_o, irq_hi_o, events_o, ch_0_o..ch_3_o Expected guard: privilege or policy guard before programming timer/event generation if interrupts/events can affect protected execution contexts Observed behavior: apb_timer_unit and apb_adv_timer expose APB ports and interrupt/event/channel outputs, and periph_bus_wrap maps timer and advanced timer ranges. The provided observations do not include register write enables, output-generation logic, downstream consumers, or guard signals. Path: APB requester -> timer/advanced timer control -> interrupt/event outputs Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Programming timers/events could affect interrupts or external timer channels, but whether those outputs influence protected contexts is not established by the provided evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need APB write decode for timer registers, interrupt/event generation logic, downstream consumers, and any privilege/lock/policy guard.

findings.summary: The SoC interconnect aggregates FC CPU, debug, uDMA and other masters and routes them using address ranges; no requester-aware access-control or privilege/security attribute check is visible in the inspected routing logic.
findings.vulnerability_category: route/read/write / s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, APB bridge/peripheral bus, L2/TCDM and target decode regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: FC CPU instruction/data, debug/JTAG, uDMA, HWPE/AXI and other interconnect masters Operation: route/read/write Object: s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, APB bridge/peripheral bus, L2/TCDM and target decode regions Expected guard: requester-aware access-control or address map preserving subject identity so debug/DMA/unprivileged CPU cannot access protected target ranges unintentionally Observed behavior: soc_interconnect defines address ranges for APB, AXI/cluster, ROM, private L2 and TCDM. FC instruction, FC data, debug and uDMA requesters are concatenated into common master vectors; l2_tcdm_demux instances receive only request/address/write/data/byte-enable/aux and shared PER/TCDM range constants. The visible logic is address-based and does not show subject-specific deny/range checks or privilege/security attributes. Path: SoC interconnect requesters -> l2_tcdm_demux / XBAR_L2 / peripheral bridge -> ROM/L2/SCM/APB targets Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug, DMA, CPU and other masters may be able to access the same protected memory/peripheral regions whenever they issue matching addresses, undermining isolation between requesters.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need confirmation whether privilege/security attributes exist elsewhere on interfaces or CPU-side PMP blocks; no such attributes are visible in the provided snippets.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
